description: Convert single-tenant internal tool to multi-tenant SaaS. Each client (org) gets their own sellers, users, and operations. Shared schema with org_id on all tables. Self-service signup with email+password+company. Super-admin panel for platform owner. Stripe billing integration (ready but deferred).

userStories.title: Database migration: orgs table and org_id columns
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to create the orgs table and add org_id to all existing tables so that data can be isolated per organization.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migration file app/db/migrations/007_multi_tenant.sql

Table `orgs` created with columns: id (UUID PK DEFAULT gen_random_uuid()), name (TEXT NOT NULL), email (TEXT UNIQUE NOT NULL), active (BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true), stripe_customer_id (TEXT UNIQUE), stripe_subscription_id (TEXT UNIQUE), payment_active (BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false), created_at (TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now()), updated_at (TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT now())
Add org_id UUID column (nullable initially) with FK REFERENCES orgs(id) ON DELETE CASCADE to: users, copy_sellers, user_permissions
Add org_id UUID column (nullable initially) with FK REFERENCES orgs(id) ON DELETE SET NULL to: copy_logs, compat_logs, api_debug_logs, auth_logs
Add is_super_admin BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false column to users table
Add email TEXT column to users table
Create index idx_orgs_email ON orgs(email)
Create index idx_orgs_stripe_customer ON orgs(stripe_customer_id)
Create indexes idx_{table}_org ON each table for the org_id column
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use IF NOT EXISTS for all CREATE TABLE and CREATE INDEX. Use ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS for ALTER TABLE. Check existing migrations in app/db/migrations/ for style (001 through 006 exist). Supabase uses PostgreSQL. Do NOT add NOT NULL to org_id yet — that happens after data backfill in US-002. Do NOT change unique constraints yet — US-002 handles that.

userStories.title: Database migration: backfill existing data and finalize constraints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to migrate existing data to a 'Lever Money' org and enforce org-scoped constraints so that the system is ready for multi-tenancy.
acceptanceCriteria: Create migration file app/db/migrations/008_backfill_org_data.sql
INSERT INTO orgs with a deterministic UUID '00000000-0000-0000-0000-1ev3rm0ney01', name='Lever Money', email='eryk@levermoney.com.br', active=true, payment_active=true
UPDATE all rows in users, copy_sellers, copy_logs, compat_logs, api_debug_logs, auth_logs, user_permissions to SET org_id = the Lever Money UUID WHERE org_id IS NULL
UPDATE users SET is_super_admin = true WHERE username = 'eryk'
ALTER TABLE users ALTER COLUMN org_id SET NOT NULL
ALTER TABLE copy_sellers ALTER COLUMN org_id SET NOT NULL
ALTER TABLE user_permissions ALTER COLUMN org_id SET NOT NULL
Drop old unique constraint on users(username) and create new UNIQUE INDEX idx_users_org_username ON users(org_id, username)
Create UNIQUE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email) WHERE email IS NOT NULL
Drop old unique constraint on copy_sellers(slug) and create new UNIQUE INDEX idx_copy_sellers_org_slug ON copy_sellers(org_id, slug)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This migration MUST run after 007_multi_tenant.sql. Use IF NOT EXISTS for indexes. The deterministic UUID uses hex chars only (no special chars). For dropping constraints safely, use: ALTER TABLE users DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS users_username_key; and ALTER TABLE copy_sellers DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS copy_sellers_slug_key;. Log tables (copy_logs, compat_logs, api_debug_logs, auth_logs) keep org_id nullable for edge cases.

userStories.title: Config: add Stripe settings to app/config.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Stripe configuration variables available so that billing endpoints can use them later.
acceptanceCriteria: Add stripe_secret_key: str = '' to Settings class in app/config.py
Add stripe_webhook_secret: str = '' to Settings class
Add stripe_price_id: str = '' to Settings class
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Simple change — just add 3 fields to the existing Settings(BaseSettings) class in app/config.py. They default to empty string so they're optional. No other files need changes.

userStories.title: Backend: update require_user to return org_id and add require_super_admin
userStories.description: As a developer, I want auth dependencies to include org context so that all downstream code can scope queries by org.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify require_user() in app/routers/auth.py to return org_id, is_super_admin, and email in the user dict
The user dict returned is: {id, username, email, role, org_id, is_super_admin, can_run_compat, permissions}
Add new async dependency require_super_admin() that calls require_user() then checks is_super_admin is True, raises HTTPException 403 with detail 'Acesso restrito ao super-admin' if not

Add new async dependency require_active_org() that calls require_user(), fetches org from orgs table by org_id, raises HTTPException 403 if org.active is False. For super_admin users, skip the check. Add a commented-out block for payment_active check with comment '# Enable when Stripe is configured'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In require_user(), the current code fetches from users table and _get_user_permissions(). Just add the new fields from the users row: user['org_id'], user.get('is_super_admin', False), user.get('email'). The users table already has these columns from US-001/002. require_active_org uses get_db().table('orgs').select('active, payment_active').eq('id', user['org_id']).single().execute().

userStories.title: Backend: signup endpoint
userStories.description: As a new customer, I want to sign up with email, password and company name so that I can start using the platform.
acceptanceCriteria: Add POST /api/auth/signup endpoint in app/routers/auth.py
Request model SignupRequest with fields: email (str), password (str), company_name (str)
Validate email is not empty and doesn't already exist in users table (return 409 'Email ja cadastrado')
Validate password is at least 6 chars (return 400 'Senha deve ter pelo menos 6 caracteres')
Validate company_name is not empty (return 400 'Nome da empresa e obrigatorio')
Create org row: name=company_name, email=email, active=true, payment_active=true
Create user row: email=email, username=email (use email as username), password_hash=bcrypt(password), role='admin', can_run_compat=true, org_id=org.id, is_super_admin=false
Create session token (same pattern as login: secrets.token_urlsafe(32), 7 day expiry)
Return {token, user: {id, username, email, role, can_run_compat}, org: {id, name}}
Log action='signup' to auth_logs with user_id, username, and org_id
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the exact same patterns from the existing login endpoint in auth.py for password hashing (bcrypt.hashpw), session creation (user_sessions insert), and token generation (secrets.token_urlsafe(32)). The new org has payment_active=true initially — billing enforcement is deferred. Username is set to email for simplicity.

userStories.title: Backend: update login to accept email
userStories.description: As a user, I want to log in with my email so that the login flow matches the signup flow.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify LoginRequest model in app/routers/auth.py to have field 'email' instead of 'username'
POST /api/auth/login looks up user by email field first
If email lookup fails, fallback to looking up by username (backward compatibility for existing users who may not have email set)
The rest of the login flow (bcrypt verify, session creation, auth_logs) stays the same
GET /api/auth/me returns org info: add org_name by joining/querying orgs table using user's org_id
The /me response adds: email, org_id, org_name, is_super_admin to the returned dict
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In the login endpoint, first try: db.table('users').select('*').eq('email', req.email).execute(). If not result.data, fallback: db.table('users').select('*').eq('username', req.email).execute(). This lets existing users (who have username but no email) still log in. For /me, after getting user, query: db.table('orgs').select('name').eq('id', user['org_id']).single().execute() and add org_name to response.

userStories.title: Backend: thread org_id through ml_api.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all ML API functions to accept org_id so that seller token lookups are scoped per organization.
acceptanceCriteria: Add org_id: str parameter to _get_token() and add .eq('org_id', org_id) to the copy_sellers query

Add org_id: str parameter to get_item(), get_item_description(), get_item_compatibilities(), create_item(), set_item_description(), set_item_compatibilities(), update_item() — all pass org_id to _get_token()
Add org_id: str parameter to search_items_by_sku() — add .eq('org_id', org_id) to copy_sellers query AND pass org_id to _get_token()
Add org_id: str parameter to copy_item_compatibilities() — pass org_id to _get_token()
Add org_id: str parameter to get_seller_official_store_id() — add .eq('org_id', org_id) to copy_sellers query AND pass org_id to _get_token()
Add org_id: str parameter to exchange_code() and fetch_user_info() — these don't need it for queries but keep consistent signature (no query changes needed)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a mechanical change: add org_id parameter to every public function, thread it to _get_token() calls. The _get_token() function at line ~112 queries copy_sellers by slug — add .eq('org_id', org_id) to that query. search_items_by_sku() at line ~343 also queries copy_sellers directly — add .eq('org_id', org_id) there too. get_seller_official_store_id() at line ~196 queries copy_sellers — add org_id filter. exchange_code() and fetch_user_info() don't query sellers so org_id is just passed through for API consistency. IMPORTANT: After this change, all callers in item_copier.py, compat_copier.py, and routers will fail typecheck until updated in subsequent stories.

userStories.title: Backend: thread org_id through item_copier.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the copy service to pass org_id to all ML API calls so that seller lookups are org-scoped.
acceptanceCriteria: Add org_id: str parameter to copy_single_item() and pass it to all ml_api function calls (get_item, get_item_description, get_item_compatibilities, create_item, set_item_description, copy_item_compatibilities, get_seller_official_store_id)
Add org_id: str parameter to copy_items() and pass to copy_single_item()
Add org_id: str parameter to copy_with_dimensions() and pass to update_item() and copy_single_item()
Add org_id field to _log_api_debug() function and include it in the insert row dict
All copy_logs inserts in copy_items() include org_id in the insert dict
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: copy_single_item() is the main function (~line 617). It calls: get_item(), get_item_description(), get_item_compatibilities(), create_item(), set_item_description(), copy_item_compatibilities(), get_seller_official_store_id(). Add org_id= to each call. copy_items() (~line 896) creates copy_logs rows — add 'org_id': org_id to log_insert dict. _log_api_debug() (~line 40) inserts into api_debug_logs — add org_id to the row dict and the function signature.

userStories.title: Backend: thread org_id through compat_copier.py
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the compat service to pass org_id to all ML API calls so that seller lookups are org-scoped.
acceptanceCriteria: Add org_id: str parameter to search_sku_all_sellers() and add .eq('org_id', org_id) to the copy_sellers query, pass org_id to search_items_by_sku() and get_item() calls
Add org_id: str parameter to _resolve_source_seller() and add .eq('org_id', org_id) to the copy_sellers query, pass org_id to get_item() calls
Add org_id: str parameter to copy_compat_to_targets() and pass org_id to _resolve_source_seller(), get_item_compatibilities(), copy_item_compatibilities(). Include org_id in compat_logs inserts
Pass org_id to _log_api_debug() calls in the error handler
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: search_sku_all_sellers() at line ~22 queries copy_sellers — add .eq('org_id', org_id). It calls search_items_by_sku() and get_item() — pass org_id. _resolve_source_seller() at line ~77 queries copy_sellers — add .eq('org_id', org_id). copy_compat_to_targets() at line ~90 writes to compat_logs — add org_id to the insert dicts.

userStories.title: Backend: org-scope auth_ml.py (OAuth + sellers)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the OAuth flow and seller management to be org-scoped so that each org manages their own ML connections.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/ml/install requires authentication via Depends(require_user), extracts org_id from user, encodes org_id in OAuth state parameter as 'org_{org_id}', returns JSON {redirect_url: '...'} instead of RedirectResponse
GET /api/ml/callback parses org_id from state parameter (strip 'org_' prefix), includes org_id in copy_sellers insert/update, scopes existing seller lookup by org_id
GET /api/sellers adds .eq('org_id', user['org_id']) to copy_sellers query. Uses Depends(require_user) instead of Depends(require_admin) so any authenticated user can see their org's sellers
DELETE /api/sellers/{slug} adds .eq('org_id', user['org_id']) to the update query to prevent cross-org deletion
The callback success page still works (HTML response)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Current install() at line ~21 returns RedirectResponse. Change to return JSON: {redirect_url: url}. The frontend will open this URL. Add 'from app.routers.auth import require_user' if not already imported. In callback() at line ~36, parse state: if state.startswith('org_'): org_id = state[4:]. The existing seller lookup by ml_user_id needs .eq('org_id', org_id) because the same ML user could be connected by different orgs. The callback does NOT require auth (it's a redirect from ML). list_sellers currently uses require_admin — change to require_user so operators can also see sellers.

userStories.title: Backend: org-scope copy.py router
userStories.description: As a developer, I want copy endpoints to be org-scoped so that each org only sees and operates on their own data.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract org_id = user['org_id'] in all endpoints that use Depends(require_user)
Pass org_id to copy_items() and copy_with_dimensions() calls
_resolve_item_seller() accepts org_id parameter, adds .eq('org_id', org_id) to copy_sellers query
preview_item() passes org_id to _resolve_item_seller() and get_item() calls
copy_logs() adds .eq('org_id', user['org_id']) filter. For super-admin (user.get('is_super_admin')), skip the org_id filter to see all logs
retry_dimensions() scopes copy_logs lookup by org_id
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In copy_anuncios() at line ~84, extract org_id = user['org_id'] and pass to copy_items(). In copy_with_dims(), same. In preview_item(), pass org_id to _resolve_item_seller() and get_item(). In copy_logs(), the current admin check is 'if user["role"] != "admin"' — change to also check is_super_admin: super-admins see all, org admins see their org, operators see their own. _resolve_item_seller() queries copy_sellers — add .eq('org_id', org_id).

userStories.title: Backend: org-scope compat.py router
userStories.description: As a developer, I want compat endpoints to be org-scoped so that each org only sees their own data.
acceptanceCriteria: Extract org_id = user['org_id'] in all endpoints
_resolve_item_seller() accepts org_id, adds .eq('org_id', org_id) to copy_sellers query
preview_item() passes org_id to _resolve_item_seller(), get_item(), get_item_compatibilities(). Scopes first-seller fallback query by org_id
search_sku() passes org_id to search_sku_all_sellers()
copy_compat() includes org_id in compat_logs insert, passes org_id to copy_compat_to_targets()
compat_logs() adds .eq('org_id', user['org_id']) filter. Super-admin sees all
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Same pattern as US-011 for copy.py. The _resolve_item_seller() in compat.py is a duplicate of the one in copy.py — both need the same change. preview_item() at line ~52 has a fallback: db.table('copy_sellers').select('slug').limit(1).execute() — add .eq('org_id', org_id) and .eq('active', True) to this. copy_compat() at line ~161 inserts into compat_logs — add org_id to log_insert dict.

userStories.title: Backend: org-scope admin_users.py router
userStories.description: As a developer, I want user management endpoints scoped by org so that each org admin only manages their own users.
acceptanceCriteria: list_users() adds .eq('org_id', user['org_id']) to users query
create_user() includes org_id=user['org_id'] in the insert dict. Checks username uniqueness within org (not globally)
update_user() verifies target user belongs to same org before updating
delete_user() verifies target user belongs to same org before deleting
get_user_permissions() scopes copy_sellers query by .eq('org_id', user['org_id'])
update_user_permissions() adds org_id to the upsert dict
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In list_users() at line ~48, add .eq('org_id', user['org_id']). In create_user() at line ~57, add 'org_id': user['org_id'] to new_user dict. For uniqueness check, change .eq('username', req.username) to also .eq('org_id', user['org_id']). In update_user()/delete_user(), first fetch the target user and verify target_user['org_id'] == user['org_id'], return 404 if not. In get_user_permissions(), the sellers query at line ~133 should add .eq('org_id', user['org_id']).

userStories.title: Backend: super-admin router
userStories.description: As the platform owner, I want API endpoints to see all organizations, their usage stats, and toggle their active status.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new file app/routers/super_admin.py with APIRouter(prefix='/api/super', tags=['super-admin'])
All endpoints use Depends(require_super_admin) from app.routers.auth
GET /api/super/orgs returns list of all orgs, each with: id, name, email, active, payment_active, created_at, user_count, seller_count, copy_count (last 30 days), compat_count (last 30 days)
PUT /api/super/orgs/{org_id} accepts {active?: bool, payment_active?: bool} and updates the org
Register the router in app/main.py: app.include_router(super_admin.router)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: For the stats, use separate count queries: db.table('users').select('id', count='exact').eq('org_id', org_id).execute() → result.count. Same pattern for copy_sellers (add .eq('active', True)), copy_logs (add date filter for last 30 days using .gte('created_at', thirty_days_ago.isoformat())), compat_logs. Import require_super_admin from app.routers.auth. Import get_db from app.db.supabase. Use datetime for 30-day calculation.

userStories.title: Backend: billing router (Stripe)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want billing endpoints ready so that Stripe can be configured later without code changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Create new file app/routers/billing.py with APIRouter(prefix='/api/billing', tags=['billing'])
Add 'stripe' to requirements.txt

POST /api/billing/create-checkout (Depends(require_user), must be org admin): creates Stripe Checkout Session with mode='subscription', returns {checkout_url}. Creates Stripe customer if org has no stripe_customer_id. Saves stripe_customer_id to org.
POST /api/billing/create-portal (Depends(require_user), must be org admin): creates Stripe Customer Portal session, returns {portal_url}. Returns 400 if org has no stripe_customer_id.

POST /api/billing/webhook (no auth): verifies Stripe signature, handles events: checkout.session.completed (set payment_active=true, save subscription_id), customer.subscription.deleted (set payment_active=false), customer.subscription.updated (check status)
GET /api/billing/status (Depends(require_user)): returns org's {payment_active, stripe_subscription_id} for current user's org
Register the router in app/main.py
If stripe_secret_key is empty, all endpoints return 503 with 'Billing not configured'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Import stripe, set stripe.api_key = settings.stripe_secret_key. For create-checkout: stripe.checkout.Session.create(mode='subscription', line_items=[{price: settings.stripe_price_id, quantity: 1}], customer=stripe_customer_id or None, customer_email=org_email, success_url=f'{settings.base_url}?billing=success', cancel_url=f'{settings.base_url}?billing=cancel', client_reference_id=str(org_id)). For webhook: stripe.Webhook.construct_event(payload, sig_header, settings.stripe_webhook_secret). The webhook endpoint must accept raw body — use Request object and await request.body(). For admin check within require_user: if user['role'] != 'admin': raise HTTPException(403).

userStories.title: Frontend: update types and useAuth for multi-tenant
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the frontend types and auth hook updated so that all components have access to org context.
acceptanceCriteria: In frontend/src/lib/api.ts: add Org interface {id: string, name: string, email: string, active: boolean, payment_active: boolean, created_at: string}
In frontend/src/lib/api.ts: add OrgWithStats interface extending Org with {user_count: number, seller_count: number, copy_count: number, compat_count: number}
In frontend/src/hooks/useAuth.ts: update AuthUser interface to add: email: string, org_id: string, org_name: string, is_super_admin: boolean
In useAuth.ts: add signup() method that calls POST /api/auth/signup with {email, password, company_name}, stores token, fetches /me, returns boolean success
In useAuth.ts: fetchMe() parses new fields from /api/auth/me response (org_id, org_name, is_super_admin, email)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In useAuth.ts, the AuthUser interface is at line ~12. Add the 4 new fields. The signup() method follows the same pattern as login(): POST to API, store token, call fetchMe(). The /api/auth/me endpoint now returns org_name (from US-006). For the api.ts types, add them after the existing interfaces.

userStories.title: Frontend: signup page
userStories.description: As a new customer, I want a signup page so that I can create an account for my company.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/pages/Signup.tsx with fields: Email (email input), Senha (password input), Nome da Empresa (text input)
Submit button 'Criar Conta' calls auth.signup(email, password, companyName)
On success, redirects to main app (parent handles via auth state change)
On failure, shows inline error message with same styling as Login.tsx
Link at bottom: 'Ja tem conta? Entrar' that navigates to login view
Follow existing design system (CSS variables, card styling from Login.tsx)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Copy the structure of Login.tsx for styling. The signup page uses the same card layout, input styling, and error display pattern. The auth.signup() method was added in US-016. The parent App.tsx will handle routing between Login/Signup views in US-019.

userStories.title: Frontend: update Login page for email
userStories.description: As a user, I want the login page to accept email and link to signup.
acceptanceCriteria: Change 'Usuario' label to 'Email' in Login.tsx
Change input type from 'text' to 'email'
The login call sends {email: value, password: value} instead of {username: value, password: value}
Add link below the form: 'Ainda nao tem conta? Cadastre-se' that calls an onNavigateToSignup callback prop
Keep the 'Acesso Admin' master password flow for backward compat (it still sends username field)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In Login.tsx, the login function currently sends {username, password}. Change to {email: username_value, password}. The onNavigateToSignup prop will be passed from App.tsx. Keep the admin-promote flow as-is since it's used for bootstrap. Add the signup link with the same styling as other text links in the app.

userStories.title: Frontend: App.tsx routing for signup, super-admin tab, org display
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to handle signup routing, show org name in header, and show super-admin tab when applicable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add authView state ('login' | 'signup') for unauthenticated routing. Default to 'login'
When !isAuthenticated: show Login if authView='login', Signup if authView='signup'
Pass onNavigateToSignup callback to Login that sets authView='signup'
Pass onNavigateToLogin callback to Signup (for the 'Ja tem conta?' link)
In header, display org name next to username: '{username} - {org_name}'
If user.is_super_admin, add 'Plataforma' tab to visibleTabs that shows SuperAdminPage
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In App.tsx, add 'super' to the View type. The visibleTabs useMemo already conditionally adds tabs — add: if (u.is_super_admin) tabs.push('super'). For the unauthenticated view, wrap the Login/Signup toggle in the existing !isAuthenticated block. The SuperAdminPage component will be created in US-020. For now, render a placeholder div if the component doesn't exist yet.

userStories.title: Frontend: super-admin page
userStories.description: As the platform owner, I want a page to see all organizations, their payment status, and usage stats.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/pages/SuperAdminPage.tsx

Fetch GET /api/super/orgs on mount, display table with columns: Empresa, Email, Status (Ativo/Inativo badge), Pagamento (Ativo/Inativo badge), Usuarios, Sellers, Copias (30d), Compats (30d), Criado em
Each row has a toggle button to activate/deactivate org (PUT /api/super/orgs/{id})
Use same table styling as CopyPage logs table (th/td styles, StatusBadge pattern)
Follow existing design system (Card component from CopyPage, CSS variables)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Import Card from './CopyPage' (it's already exported). Use the same table styling pattern (th with uppercase labels, td with padding). For the toggle, use a simple button that calls PUT with {active: !current_active}. Refresh the list after toggle. The API returns OrgWithStats shape from US-016 types. Format dates with toLocaleString('pt-BR').

userStories.title: Frontend: billing page and payment banner
userStories.description: As an org admin, I want to see billing status and subscribe via Stripe, and see a banner if payment is pending.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/pages/BillingPage.tsx accessible from Admin tab as a sub-section
Show current billing status from GET /api/billing/status: payment active/inactive
If payment inactive: show 'Assinar' button that calls POST /api/billing/create-checkout and redirects to checkout_url
If payment active: show 'Gerenciar Assinatura' button that calls POST /api/billing/create-portal and redirects to portal_url
In App.tsx: if user is org admin and org.payment_active is false, show a subtle banner below header: 'Assinatura pendente' with link to billing
If billing endpoints return 503 (not configured), hide the billing section entirely
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The billing status comes from GET /api/billing/status. If it returns 503, set a 'billingAvailable=false' state and hide the section. For the Assinar button, POST /api/billing/create-checkout returns {checkout_url} — use window.location.href = checkout_url to redirect. Same for portal. Add BillingPage as a sub-tab in the Admin section alongside Sellers and Usuarios. The payment banner in App.tsx should be a simple div with warning styling.

userStories.title: Frontend: update Admin page for OAuth install flow
userStories.description: As an org admin, I want the ML seller install to work with the new authenticated flow.
acceptanceCriteria: In Admin.tsx (or wherever the ML install link is), change from direct link to fetching GET /api/ml/install with auth headers
The API now returns {redirect_url} — open it with window.open(redirect_url, '_blank') or window.location.href
If the fetch fails with 401, show error message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The current Admin.tsx likely has an <a href> link to /api/ml/install. Since this endpoint now requires auth (X-Auth-Token header) and returns JSON instead of redirecting, we need to fetch it programmatically. Use fetch() with headers() from props, parse JSON, then window.open() the redirect_url. This is a small change — just update the install button/link handler.
